mission.title: crossplane: Running Crossplane on hostNetwork
mission.description: Running Crossplane on hostNetwork. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

Running Crossplane on an AWS EKS cluster with the Calico CNI leads to multiple problems:

- Every webhook (validating/mutating) must be run with `hostNetwork: true`.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Running Crossplane on hostNetwork
steps.description:
### Description of your changes

When running crossplane (and providers/functions) in the host network the need to configure ports arises.

This MR has two parts:

1. Make crossplane ports (service/webhook, health probe, metrics) configurable
2. Allow `DeploymentRuntimeConfig` to override the
```yaml
hostNetwork: true
metrics:
  # -- Enable Prometheus path, port and scrape annotations and expose port 8080 for both the Crossplane and RBAC Manager pods.
  enabled: false
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Running Crossplane on hostNetwork" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Description of your changes

When running crossplane (and providers/functions) in the host network the need to configure ports arises.

This MR has two parts:

1. Make crossplane ports (service/webhook, health probe, metrics) configurable
2. Allow `DeploymentRuntimeConfig` to override the default ports (`metrics`, `webhook`, `grpc`) in both `Deployment` and `Service`.
codeSnippets:
hostNetwork: true
metrics:
  # -- Enable Prometheus path, port and scrape annotations and expose port 8080 for both the Crossplane and RBAC Manager pods.
  enabled: false

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
